pattern: The value domain is tiny, so mark every integer covered by some interval in a small boolean array and confirm each point in [left, right] is marked.
hints: All numbers stay within 1..50, so you can afford to test each integer in the query range directly.
For a fixed integer x, it is covered when some interval has start <= x <= end.
Return false the moment a point in [left, right] is uncovered, otherwise true.

edge_inputs.description: contiguous coverage of the query range
a single point outside all intervals
single interval exactly equals the query
gap between two intervals leaves a point uncovered
left equals right inside an interval
query starts below the smallest interval

stress.description: random small intervals and queries within the 1..50 domain
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    ranges = []
    for _ in range(k):
        a = rng.randint(1, 50)
        b = rng.randint(a, 50)
        ranges.append([a, b])
    l = rng.randint(1, 50)
    r = rng.randint(l, 50)
    return [ranges, l, r]

stress.description: narrow single-point queries to stress boundary detection
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 10)
    ranges = []
    for _ in range(k):
        a = rng.randint(1, 50)
        b = rng.randint(a, 50)
        ranges.append([a, b])
    x = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [ranges, x, x]
